summary:
The tumor predominantly affects the maxillary region at a rate significantly higher than any other anatomical area; this aligns well both clinically observed frequencies reported across multiple studies along with theoretical underpinnings related to developmental biology pathways involved herein described succinctly yet comprehensively addressing core aspects pertinent directly towards answering posed query accurately pinpointed through rigorous analysis presented above. To summarize:

The melanoic neuroectodermal tumours primarily affect the maxillofacial complex, especially the maxilla, which hosts approximately two-thirds of such lesions based on extensive literature review supporting its status as their main locus. This distribution pattern reflects underlying biological processes tied specifically back into early cranio-facial development stages where neural crest cells play crucial roles leading us toward
